entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025: Future-proof your integration investment with AI (BTP2121)
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, My name is Arti Gopalan and I am a lead product manager based out of India for SAP Integration Suite, which is an enterprise-grade integration platform-as-a-service that helps businesses seamlessly connect and integrate their applications, data, and processes.

My session: Strategy Talk - Future-proof your integration investment with AI - BTP2121 on May 20th, 4:00 PM - 4:20 PM EDT as part of the SAP Sapphire 2025 in Orlando.

What you will learn about: 

As someone deeply involved in the evolution of enterprise integration at SAP, I am excited to be at SAP Sapphire once again this year to represent SAP Integration Suite. You can meet me at the booth – Move from SAP PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite - BTP2786. Location: Platform, 157. I will also be presenting a strategy talk on the innovation areas being focused by our team to future-proof your integration investments.

There is an ever-evolving demand for interconnected ecosystems. Ensuring that applications, systems, and processes are connected and are able to respond in real-time has become table stakes across organizations. There is a focused need to support agile integrations and accelerate responsiveness that allows adapting to the dynamic business requirements and the constant influx of new technologies. Discovering process inefficiencies, automating enterprise-wide processes and accelerating innovations across the organization and beyond have become the drivers.

In this session, I will be providing an in-depth look the how we are leveraging Artificial Intelligence to augment the core capabilities of SAP Integration Suite. I'll be illustrating how the intelligent application of AI is poised to fundamentally reshape how we approach integration, leading to significant gains in developer productivity and operational efficiencies through:

  • Intelligent Assistance in Integration Design
  • AI-Driven Transformation and Mapping
  • Optimizing existing integrations
  • Automated Anomaly Detection, Remediation and Trend Prediction

If you're responsible for the critical task of designing, implementing, and maintaining resilient and scalable integration solutions, this session will be interesting for you. I look forward to a rich and engaging discussion.

Other related sessions I recommend:  

See all SAP Integration Suite sessions available to you in Orlando: customer use cases, strategy talks, road map reviews and much more is in store for you - do not miss it!

Browse the catalog & register ➡️ https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/flow/sap/so25/catalog-inperson/page/catalog?tab.sessionplanned=1692641568884001cZ9d&search=%22SAP%20Integration%20Suite%22

Now let’s hear from you: 

What specific capability or future direction of SAP Integration Suite are you most eager to explore and would like to hear more about? Please answer by adding a comment in the comments section.

Register for SAP Sapphire 2025 and join me at the session BTP2121: Future-proof your integration investment with AI. I'm eager to connect with you there.

See you soon.

entry.title: Rethink ALM: Moving to SAP Cloud ALM as a Clean Core Strategy for Application Lifecycle Management
entry.content:
For over 20 years, SAP customers have relied on SAP Solution Manager to manage the complexity of their SAP landscapes. But with the rapid shift to the cloud, modular architectures, and continuous innovation cycles, a new paradigm is emerging - one that demands a leaner, cleaner, and more agile approach to Application Lifecycle Management (ALM).

Enter SAP Cloud ALM.

This is not a like-for-like replacement for Solution Manager. It’s something much more strategic. It represents a clean core transformation of your ALM processes themselves - a chance to simplify, refocus, and realign your ALM strategy with the realities of the cloud and the future of business innovation.

 

Beyond Tools: ALM in the era of clean core

SAP’s clean core strategy encourages customers to keep their ERP core as standard as possible - minimizing custom code, reducing technical debt, and ensuring cloud readiness. But this principle isn’t limited to ERP.

The move to SAP Cloud ALM is your clean core strategy for your ALM processes.

Instead of lifting and shifting every process, integration, and customization from SAP Solution Manager, SAP Cloud ALM challenges you to ask:

  • What ALM capabilities are essential?
  • Where can we standardize?
  • How do we reduce operational overhead and increase value delivery?

It’s an opportunity to strip back unnecessary complexity and rebuild your ALM approach based on what the business truly needs today - not what was built for yesterday’s IT environments.

 

Why SAP Cloud ALM requires rethinking ALM processes

It’s important to acknowledge a key reality: SAP Cloud ALM does not replicate the full breadth of functionality found in SAP Solution Manager. That’s by design.

Rather than duplicating every feature, SAP has built SAP Cloud ALM to:

  • Deliver core capabilities out of the box
  • Support standardized, best-practice processes
  • Prioritize business value and cloud scalability

This means customers moving to SAP Cloud ALM must take a step back and evaluate which ALM processes are truly needed - and which are not.

In doing so, you’re not just migrating tools. You’re engaging in a strategic redesign of your ALM function, aligning it with:

  • Modern cloud landscapes
  • End-to-end business process monitoring
  • Agile project delivery and fit-to-standard implementations

 

The Value of clean core ALM with SAP Cloud ALM

So, what does this clean core approach to ALM actually deliver? Here are the key strategic benefits:

Simplified ALM footprint

  • No more managing on-premise infrastructure or maintaining legacy configurations.
  • Everything is delivered as-a-service, with automatic updates and minimal setup.

Focus on what matters

  • Prioritize the capabilities that drive business outcomes: implementation tracking, test orchestration, business process monitoring, and operations analytics.
  • Eliminate or de-emphasize legacy processes that no longer fit a modern, agile delivery model.

Built for SAP’s cloud future

  • Native integration with SAP Signavio, SAP LeanIX, and Tricentis Test Automation within the so-called "integrated toolchain"
  • Implementation and operations support for a large number of SAP solutions.
  • Direct alignment with SAP’s roadmap and innovation cycles - ensuring future compatibility and continuous improvement.

Lower TCO and faster time to value

  • SAP Cloud ALM is included in most SAP cloud subscriptions - no additional license cost.
  • Faster onboarding, no upgrade cycles, and reduced operational effort.

Foundation for agile, modular IT

  • SAP Cloud ALM supports agile implementation and deployment patterns.
  • Encourages modular design and decentralized governance—perfect for hybrid and composable business environments.

 

Clean Core ALM = A strategic reset

Think of the move to SAP Cloud ALM not as a downgrade or a compromise, but as a reset.

It’s a shift away from overly customized, maintenance-heavy ALM practices - and a move toward standardized, insight-driven, and value-focused application lifecycle management.

Much like the clean core mindset encourages you to remove unnecessary ERP modifications, SAP Cloud ALM encourages you to:

  • Simplify ALM governance
  • Standardize implementation and operations
  • Drive better outcomes with less effort

It’s a strategic enabler - not just for technical teams, but for business transformation itself.

 

The bottom line: Transform ALM with a clean core mindset

SAP Cloud ALM isn’t meant to do everything SAP Solution Manager did. It’s meant to do what today’s businesses actually need - in a lean, efficient, and future-ready way.

That’s why this transition should be seen as a clean core strategy for ALM:

  • Clean: Streamline and eliminate complexity
  • Core: Focus on essential processes that deliver business value
  • Strategy: Align with SAP’s innovation roadmap and enable continuous evolution

By embracing SAP Cloud ALM, you’re not just choosing a new tool - you’re choosing a new way to manage the lifecycle of your SAP solutions: lighter, faster, and more in tune with the demands of cloud business models.

This is your moment to reimagine ALM—not as a legacy overhead, but as a strategic function that powers transformation.

 

What is your take on the transition to SAP Cloud ALM as a clean core approach for your ALM processes? Let me know in the comments below!

 

Learn More

  • Transition Center - all resources and information for moving to SAP Cloud ALM
  • SAP Cloud ALM Overview
  • SAP Cloud ALM Roadmap Explorer

entry.title: Re: We need to change SOLMAN roles
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Greetings from SAP Support.

Please download the role in the attachment of SAP Note2250709 - SAP Solution Manager 7.2: End-User Roles and Authorizations Corrections as of SP09 and higher,  into your Solution Manager System. Proceed as follows:

  1. Download the roles in the attachment on to your PC.
  2. Unpack the zip file. 
  3. Log on as administrator (or DDIC). 
  4. Go to transaction PFCG in SAP Solution Manager. 
  5. In the menu, choose function: Upload. 
  6. Upload the roles into the system.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: cumulative delta calculation using data actions in SAC
entry.content:
I would imagine similar code like this(I haven't tested it at all): 

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = BASEMEMBER([d/Date], "[2025]")
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "IR_Shift"

VARIABLEMEMBER #YTD_SHIFT OF [d/Measures]

FOREACH [d/Date] ASC
  DATA() = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Refreshed_rates") - RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "CP_Rates")
         - RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #YTD_SHIFT, PREVIOUS(1, "MONTH", [d/Date]))
  DATA([d/Measures] = #YTD_SHIFT) = RESULTLOOKUP() + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #YTD_SHIFT, PREVIOUS(1, "MONTH", [d/Date])
ENDFOR

entry.title: Re: Segragation of roles based on Organizational values
entry.content: Analyzing organizational values and levels in segregated businesses requires separate assessments for each unit using surveys, interviews, and artifact analysis to identify shared, distinct, or conflicting values, and to compare enacted versus espoused values within their specific contexts. Similarly, map and compare organizational structures, communication flows, and decision-making processes in each. The goal isn't necessarily uniformity, but to understand the "why" behind their differences, assess alignment with strategy, identify potential synergies or conflicts, and ensure effective communication throughout the analysis to provide valuable, context-specific insights to the client.

entry.title: I am very beginner to SAP and i want to know and learn more in MM module
Re: How to transport ABAP code as inactive mode to produc...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP, you can't transport inactive ABAP code to production. The system only lets you move active and syntax-checked objects. This is intentional :  SAP wants to make sure whatever reaches QA or production won’t cause issues like dumps or inconsistencies.

Here are a few practical workarounds:

1. Use a copy

Make a temporary copy of your object (e.g., ZMY_PROG_TEST). You can transport this version while leaving your original one untouched. It’s a safe way to test or prepare for future changes without touching live code.

2. Comment it out

If the changes are minor, you can comment them out, activate the object, and then transport. Later on, uncomment when you're ready for them to go live.

3. Use a switch or flag

Wrap your new logic in an IF statement that checks a config flag or table entry. This way, you can transport the code as active, but it won’t run until you flip the switch.

4. Keep a backup

If you really want to keep your inactive version, save a local copy (or export via abapGit), then revert and activate the object before releasing it to the transport. Later, you can reapply the changes when needed.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: /DMO/FLIGHT maintain data public cloud system
entry.content:
SAPBC_DATA_GENERATOR is for the old table SFLIGHT, not for the recent /DMO/FLIGHT.

Run the class /DMO/CL_FLIGHT_DATA_GENERATOR to generate data in /DMO/FLIGHT (and other tables).

entry.title: Automating Provisioning Jobs and Job Logs using SAP Identity Provisioning (IPS) API
entry.content:
Introduction:

  • Introducing SAP Identity Provisioning (IPS) APIs and its importance in managing identity lifecycle processes across cloud and on-premise systems to automate the user provisioning process across SAP Products.
  • Known Limitations in IPS API

Current API Capabilities:

  • Triggering Jobs: The API allows you to trigger provisioning jobs programmatically, which helps in automating the process without navigating through multiple screens .
  • Retrieving Job Logs: You can also retrieve job logs to monitor the status and outcomes of the provisioning jobs

SAP IPS API Documentation

Trigger a READ Job :   https://api.sap.com/api/IPS_Proxy/resource/Jobs

 To Retrieve the Job Logs :    https://api.sap.com/api/IPS_Proxy/resource/JobLogs

IPS API Limitations:

  • Viewing Source and Target Systems: Currently, there is no public API available to view the source and target systems to get the UUID directly. you will have to copy the UUID manually from UI to trigger via API
  • Job Logs : There is no API available to get the List of Jobs with Job id. Again will you have to copy the UUID of the Job ran in your Provisioning Logs
  • Detailed Logs: The API does not provide detailed logs that include error specifics or skipped statuses
  • Scheduling : There is no API available today to trigger the schedule Job.
  • Additional APIs : There are few APIs which are not available in Public but those APIs are usage in sessions based.

Try Now to execute via API

To Trigger the READ Job for the Source System with UUID

To extract the Job Logs for the Job Id 

Below template is ready for using in Visual Studio Code and replace your actual tenant details for usage

@clientid = XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX             ### Replace
@clientsecret = XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX            ### Replace
@ias = https://XXX.accounts.ondemand.com    ### Replace


#  login
POST {{ias}}/oauth2/token?grant_type=client_credentials
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Authorization: Basic {{clientid}}:{{clientsecret}}

### Access Token for Portal Users

@accessToken = {{login.response.body.access_token}}

###  https://api.sap.com/api/IPS_Proxy/overview
###   XXXXXXXXXXXXXXX is your source System connector Id
POST {{ias}}/ips/service/publicapi/v1/startJob/XXXXXXXXXXXXXXX/jobs/READ
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Content-Type: application/json

### To Get Job Logs
#### XXXXXXX = Replace Job id selected for the Particular Job from your tenant
GET {{ias}}/ips/service/publicapi/v1/jobLogs/XXXXXXXXXXXX?logType=commonLog&action=export
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Content-Type: application/json






##########  Not available for Public - Avaiable within Browser Sessions ######## 

###
GET {{ias}}/ips/service/publicapi/v1/sourceSystems
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Content-Type: application/json

###
GET {{ias}}/ips/service/uiapi/v1/systems?mode=read&page=0&size=20
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Content-Type: application/json

###
GET {{ias}}/ips/service/uiapi/v1/systems?mode=write&page=0&size=20
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Content-Type: application/json

###
GET {{ias}}/ips/service/publicapi/v1/systems/5ff94c5b-30ea-4ba5-9ef7-ed48768e3fcc
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Content-Type: application/json

entry.title: Re: Cumulative, Opening balance calculation SAP Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Tt's a common challenge in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition when working with Key User tools like Custom CDS Views.

1. Cumulative Balance

Unfortunately, Custom CDS Views don't support SQL-style window functions (like SUM() OVER (...)), so you can't create a true running total directly in the CDS layer.

Workaround: If users are consuming the data in Fiori, Excel, or SAC reports, you can create a Custom Analytical Query on top of your CDS View and use cumulative key figures in the reporting layer. That gives you the running total behavior, but it's handled at the reporting level, not in the CDS itself.

2. Opening Balance

This is doable with Custom CDS Views:

  • Add a parameter for the period start date.
  • Filter the records to include only postings before that date.
  • Aggregate the balances.

This will give you the opening balance as of the selected start of the period.

So the summary is :

  •  Opening Balance: Yes, possible via filtering + aggregation.
  • Cumulative Balance: Not directly possible in CDS Views; best handled in SAC or Analytical Queries.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How do setup IAS login page for Onboardee and SSO pag...
entry.content:
Hello JaisonPJ1!

My recommendation would be the following: (when the default identity provider is the customer IdP)

The onboardee recives the activation email from SuccessFactors. Important that in the IPS transformation in the target system have the following code snippet:

{
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['targetUrl']",
"constant": "https://xxxxxx.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/sso?sp=https://www.successfactors.eu/xxxxxx&idp=https://xxxxxxx.accounts.ondemand.com",
"scope": "createEntity"
},

If you add this then after the registration the user will be redirected to the given URL. (The above URL is for the PWD users when you set the corporate IdP you can enable this URL)

Also the activation email should contain this link so the users can login through this if they want to login to SF again. (After they fill their data in the onboarding)

I hope this helps!

Best Regards,
Soma

entry.content:
@JaisonPJ1 @Soma5 @abdulbasit 

Yes, Its possible to skip the IAS Login page to display for users when there is a multiple Identity Provider exist in your IAS - Corporate Identity Provider.

User 1 (depending on whatever the user type X):  Display IDP Login Page 

User 2 (depending on whatever the User Type Y): Display IDP Login Page (3rd Party or IAS Login Page)

I haven't written a blog for this, may be this month with different examples will be prepared and publish it.

1. define the condition type:

2. assign the condition type to price procedure

3. assign the condition type to DN price procedure

4. determine the DN price procedure

5. change the copy control setting (DN TO Billing)

Steps:

1. VA01 create the SO:

2. VL01N create the DN

3. VF01 create the billing document

entry.title: CCM - use multiple condition tables within one condition contract
entry.content:
Dear gurus,

Is it possible to use different rebate percentages across condition tables within the same condition contract? Without summing all of the percentages when settling the contract?

I have following business scenario:

  • Rebate: Agreement with customer to pay overall 6% rebate based on business volume. However, for one specific material group (let's call it material group A), the customer receives 8% rebate.
  • Accrual: Should follow the same logic as the rebate percentages i.e., 6% overall, but for material group A we want to accrue 8%.

I am trying to achieve this by using 2 condition tables within same access sequence:

  • Cond table 1: Condition contract / Material group
  • Cond table 2: Condition contract

I am entering both REA1 and RES1 in the condition contract:

  • Cond table 1: condition contract nr / material group A = 8%
  • Cond table 2: condition contract nr = 6%

Scenario: I only have business volume of sales of material within material group A.
Expectation: Both accruals and rebate would be posted with 8%
The issue I am encountering: At time of settlement both RES1 condition records are found, ending up in 14% instead of 8%. How can I set up my condition contract to achieve my goal? Business wants to avoid to enter all of the material groups, hence, I am trying to use multiple condition tables within one contract.

For the accruals, there is no issue, we can use the exclusive indicator in the access sequence together with routine 67.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Core Data Services: New syntax for extending CDS entities
entry.content:
Hello @AndreaUS ,

Thanks for the great blog. We are currently trying to extend existing CDS views with standard SAP fields on a SAP S4 Public Cloud develop environment. However, it seems like there are only 6 CDS views which meet this prerequisite. 

  • Prerequisite: the extended entity must allow extensions. Extensions are allowed per default or can be explicitly allowed with the annotation @AbapCatalog.extensibility.extensible with the value true.

If they don't have Extensible with Developer Extensibility = yes the error below is returned. Isn't it strange that we almost can't extend existing CDS views with standard SAP fields, but we can extend with custom YY1 fields? Could you confirm that this is indeed the case?


Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Integration BDC - Success Factors
entry.content:
Hi,

We have experienced challenges integrating SuccessFactors with SAC or Datasphere. We are exploring whether BDC could help address some of these issues, particularly around connectivity and data consistency.

One of the main concerns is that in SuccessFactors, key data—such as headcount information—is dynamic and lacks built-in historical storage. As a result, we've had to implement workarounds to capture snapshots of employee data over time. Additionally, the integration with Datasphere is proving to be complex, especially due to the need for static parameter passing in OData queries, which limits flexibility.

Given these challenges, we would like to understand whether BDC can support more robust integration with SuccessFactors and potentially resolve these limitations. Does anyone have more information about the integration between BDC and Success Factors? Do the pre-built insight apps for Success Factors capture data over time? Where can I find more information?

For app "Post Outgoing Payments", there is no specific API available.
If you want to post and clear supplier-related accounts, you can try to use 'Journal Entry – Post' and 'Journal Entry – Clearing' to see if they meet your needs.

entry.title: Re: Multiple Selection F4 HELP (With Check Boxes)
entry.content:
Full working code corresponding to the given screenshots:

REPORT zdemo.

PARAMETERS material TYPE string.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR material.
  TYPES ty_dynpread_s TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF dynpread WITH EMPTY KEY.

  DATA i_marktab   TYPE ddshmarks.
  DATA i_returntab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ddshretval.

  DATA(dynpfields) = VALUE ty_dynpread_s( ( fieldname = 'MATERIAL' ) ).
  CALL FUNCTION 'DYNP_VALUES_READ'
    EXPORTING  dyname     = sy-repid
               dynumb     = sy-dynnr
    TABLES     dynpfields = dynpfields
    EXCEPTIONS OTHERS     = 9.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    material = dynpfields[ fieldname = 'MATERIAL' ]-fieldvalue.
  ENDIF.

  SELECT matnr, aenam FROM mara INTO TABLE @DATA(i_matnr) UP TO 100 ROWS.
  SPLIT material AT ',' INTO TABLE DATA(materials).
  LOOP AT materials REFERENCE INTO DATA(material_2).
    DATA(line_index) = line_index( i_matnr[ matnr = CONV #( |{ material_2->* ALPHA = IN }| ) ] ).
    IF line_index <> 0.
      INSERT line_index INTO TABLE i_marktab.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
    EXPORTING  retfield        = 'MATNR'
               dynpprog        = sy-repid
               dynpnr          = sy-dynnr
               dynprofield     = 'MATERIAL' " NB: useless when multiple_choice = 'X'
               window_title    = 'List of materials'
               value_org       = 'S'
               multiple_choice = 'X'
               mark_tab        = i_marktab
    TABLES     value_tab       = i_matnr[]
               return_tab      = i_returntab[]  " user selected table
    EXCEPTIONS parameter_error = 1
               no_values_found = 2
               OTHERS          = 3.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    material = concat_lines_of( sep   = ','
                                table = VALUE string_table( FOR <return_line> IN i_returntab
                                                            ( EXACT #( <return_line>-fieldval ) ) ) ).
    dynpfields = VALUE #( ( fieldname = 'MATERIAL' fieldvalue = material ) ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'DYNP_VALUES_UPDATE'
      EXPORTING  dyname     = sy-repid
                 dynumb     = sy-dynnr
      TABLES     dynpfields = dynpfields
      EXCEPTIONS OTHERS     = 8.
  ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori (F3074A) Timesheet V4 Worklist Enhancement
entry.content:
The solution can be this note https://me.sap.com/notes/3578788/E (unless you want to implement all the changes manually), but you need to be aware that this note will break your timesheet application. Time entry tables will not work and the ability to add either entry or quick entry will never work. Basically, the application will become fully unusable. 

So, the manual implementation could be the better way.

entry.title: Re: SAC manual import and conenction with Live data
entry.content:
Hi,

Let me try to answer your questions:

1. There are different ways to import data into a model in SAC. If you have the freedom to choice were to store the data, I would go for a secure Cloud storage solution. If you want to import the data from a on-premise solution you have to invest in setting up and maintaining a cloud connector and, in some cases, a cloud agent.

2. Joining data between import models and live models will be done on story level. Looking forward you may want to investigate the new business data cloud offering where you can utilize SAP Datasphere to bring all data together and report with SAC using a live connection.

3. Assuming you use import models you can setup a monthly schedule to import the data with different options.

Kind regards,
Martijn van Foeken | Interdobs

entry.title: Re: Data Initialization Issue in CAP Java Project with HA...
entry.content:
Hi @stefaniaZ,

A distinction must be made between test data (mocks) and initial real data:
The .csv files created in the test/data folder are only deployed when the application is not in production mode. This folder can therefore be used to store example data for tests and demonstrations.
The .csv files created in the db/data folder, on the other hand, are deployed in all environments, including production. This folder is therefore suitable for initial configuration data.

Have you tried cleaning the build artifacts: /gen, .cds deploy. There is also the --auto-undeploy parameter in cds deploy which allows you to : “Tell HDI deployer to automatically undeploy deleted resources”.

entry.title: 新功能详解：使用Housekeeping在SAP 分析云系统中高效维护数据
entry.content:
我们很高兴与大家分享一项重要更新 —— 我们升级了系统概览的内务管理功能。 

它将跟随将于5月17-19日上线的 SAC  2025.Q2 发布版本一同推出。 

在过去，我们通过系统概览提供了许多有用的信息。如今，我们弥补了又一空白。我们让信息更加可操作，即允许批量删除未使用的对象。这将通过我们之前推出的系统概览中的Housekeeping, 也就是内务管理功能得以实现。 

请点击图片查看演示视频。 

该功能现在支持有选择性地删除对象，我们距离更优的数据管理和更佳的用户体验又更近了一步。 

这对您意味着什么？ 

这一进步使您能够删除自己选定的特定对象，减少了了手动清理的工作量。但这并不止步于此。除了主要对象外，所有与之有依赖关系的相关对象也将被自动删除。这意味着您不再需要担心系统中积累的不必要且未使用的对象会拖慢系统性能。此次升级标志着我们在提供更智能、更简化的解决方案以满足您的业务需求的道路上又取得了一次成功。 

简化管理： 

只需几次点击即可删除对象及其依赖项，这极大地提升了易用性，减轻了系统管理员的负担。 

提升透明度： 

显示在特定时期内未使用的对象，为用户提供了清晰的数据概览，以便安心删除不需要的数据。用户无需再担心误删重要信息，这种透明度使他们能够更有信心地管理数据。 

降低维护工作量： 

自动检测和删除依赖项和对象，减少了手动操作。团队无需定期清理和查找依赖项，简化了维护工作，降低了管理负担。该功能实现了过程自动化，使团队能够专注于需要他们专业知识的任务。 

过去与现在对比： 

过去，用户需要手动查找系统中所有相关对象并删除，然后才能删除主要对象。这一过程不仅耗时，还容易出错，因为用户可能未能发现所有依赖项。 

重要技术背景： 

出于安全考虑，我们仅允许从系统概览内容中调用 postMessage API  (Analytics Designer API Reference Guide - postMessage ) ，进行SAC 对象的删除。 

        重要说明： 

        目前不支持删除连接和文件夹，我们计划在 2025.Q4 提供此功能。  

        出于性能优化的考量，仅支持在列表中选择少于 5,000 行进行删除操作。  

        以下对象可以删除： 

  • 模型 
  • 数据集 
  • 文件夹 （2025 Q4） 
  • 故事 
  • 分析应用 
  • 组合 
  • 数字会议室 
  • 洞察 
  • 数据操作 
  • 多步操作 
  • 主题 
  • 连接 （2025 Q4） 
  • 内容链接 

       硬删除和暂存删除之间存在区别。暂存删除会先经过回收站，而硬删除则永久删除对象。 

硬删除适用于以下对象： 

  • 模型 
  • 自定义微件 
  • 连接 （2025 Q4） 
  • 文件夹 （2025 Q4） 
  • 多步操作 
  • 数据操作 
  • 汇率表 

        其余对象采用暂存删除。 

        我们会首先查找所有依赖项并删除这些依赖项，然后再移除父对象。 

        根据所选对象及其相关对象的数量，删除过程可能需要几分钟。请耐心等待。 

我们相信，全新升级的系统概览整理功能将彻底改变您的管理负担！敬请期待更多更新和改进。 

如往常一样，我们随时为您提供帮助。如果您对新功能有任何疑问或需要帮助，请随时联系我们。 

您的成功是我们的动力！ 

原文链接：Efficient Data Maintenance in SAP Analytics Cloud ... - SAP Community 

原作者：thomas_fery

entry.title: Re: cumulative delta calculation using data actions in SAC
entry.content:
Hi @guvvala_sairam 

Please refer to another resolution of the Advanced formulas script.  

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = BASEMEMBER([d/Date], "[2025]")

VARIABLEMEMBER #Delta_CP_Rates OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Delta_Refreshed_Rates OF [d/Measures]

DATA([d/Measures] = #Delta_CP_Rates)
= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "CP_Rates")
- RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "CP_Rates", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(1))
DATA([d/Measures] = #Delta_Refreshed_Rates)
= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Refreshed_Rates")
- RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Refreshed_Rates", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(1))

DATA([d/Measures] = "IR_Shift")
= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #Delta_Refreshed_Rates)
- RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #Delta_CP_Rates)

Regards,

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Import service categories in SAP Service Cloud v2
entry.content:
Thanks for posting that question @RitSilva 

If I would be you, then I'd start to investigate on the following aspects:

  • Check Exact Name & ID of the Service Catalog: The import file must exactly match the Service Catalogue ID as defined in the system. That includes case sensitivity and spacing.

  • Ensure the Catalogue is Active: Sometimes the catalogue exists but is in draft or inactive status.

  • Correct Template / Field Mapping: In case you're using a CSV or Excel template: Make sure the column referring to the Service Catalogue is mapped correctly. Double-check column headers match what the system expects (e.g., ServiceCatalogueID or similar).

I hope one of these points will help you.

entry.title: Re: Object store secret default-object-store-secret not f...
entry.content:
Hey, I faced the issue.

i am using the SAP AI Core Toolkit in the VS Code extension.

To use the input dataset, you need to set the input artifact in the object store.

For example, i run the following command to upload my dataset:

aws s3 cp Anomalydata.csv s3://<bucket-name>/anomaly/data/Anomalydata.csv

(Make sure you run aws configure before this step.)

This will place the dataset at the anomaly/data location inside your S3 bucket. You can view it in the extension.

Then, in the toolkit (or through the Launchpad), set up the object store credentials. Name the store "default", and set the path prefix to "anomaly". Fill in the other required fields as they are.

Next, add the input artifact, and set the path to:

ai://default/data

This fixed the issue I was having with the missing secret.

You can check this video : sap ai core using sap ai core toolkit(vs code extension)

entry.title: Re: Access Control - Assessment of users, roles and their...
entry.content:
Hello @RafaMarj 

I take care about SAP GRC and also SAP Authorizations about 20 years, so I guess I can give you the answer.

Based on info provided - 2 Companies, 1 SAP architecture including SAP GRC Access Control - I would ask:

- Are mentioned businesses separated by organizational values (Company Code, etc.)?

- Does exist SAP Authorization Concept describing SAP roles, their content and who is responsible for?

- Which version of SAP GRC AC are you running?

- Do you have implemented BRM including Access Control Owners and are Role Owners defined?

- And do you have implemented Access Risk Analysis and adjusted SoD matrix?

In general, the base is SAP Authorization Concept designed in right way and followed in SAP roles, then SAP GRC AC set properly to cover SAP roles assignment strictly by approval of GRC Role Approver, UAR is implemented and also SoD matrix is set and reviewed periodically. Also 4-eyes principle is followed by related admins - SAP roles admin/s, SAP GRC admin/s.

If I take the base as set and working smoothly, then SAP roles for "gonna sold" and for "kept" are separated by organizational values and each SAP role has different GRC Role Approver. Then SAP roles for access to "kept" company data can be requested to be assigned to "gonna sold" users but if not approved by defined GRC Role Approver, no access is given. So the responsibility is on GRC Role Approver, this person is recommended to be business user for business roles. I found life saving when is GRC Role Approver a bit experienced business user - familiar with SAP role related process/es, with the content (on t-code level), also understands critical combinations of authorizations, impact of giving access rights and own responsibility for.

You can make the life of GRC Role Approvers easier - for example by creating special Access Risk that contains critical combination of "gonna sold" organizational values and of "kept" ones, moving "gonna sold" users to dedicated User Group and make this field visible in GRC request, disabling "gonna sold" users to be found on Data Source (like CUA, IdM), etc.

There are a lot of possibilities to be used via SAP GRC AC, so only depends on effort you want to spend on and also on your environment. If you are running CUA/IdM, it is easier to manage "gonna sold" users, etc.

In case of any questions don't hesitate to ask.

Have a nice day.

BR, Olga

entry.title: Google Cloud Storage Adapter for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
In many integration scenarios, access to Google Cloud Storage is an integral component. One of the latest offerings from SAP, Google Cloud Storage adapter is a Cloud Integration capability that simplifies connectivity between SAP Integration Suite and Google Cloud Storage. Leverage the full range of storage options in your integration scenarios using the Google Cloud Storage Adapter. 

Introduction to Google Cloud Storage 

Google Cloud Storage is an offering that provides an online storage web service for storing and accessing data on Google Cloud Platform infrastructure. 

Key Benefits of using Google Cloud Storage Adapter 

Google Cloud Storage Adapter has the following features: 

  • Allows you to access and interact with different entities: Buckets, Folders, and Objects. 
  • Sender Adapter provides Duplicate Check Expiration option to avoid same object being read within a predefined expiration period and offers multiple Post-processing options: Delete Object after Processing, Keep Object after Processing, and Archive Object after Processing. 
  • Archived objects can be named dynamically using Camel expressions or Camel File Expression language. 
  • Supports reading of encrypted objects with custom encryption and supports encrypted object creation. 
  • Create operation using Receiver Adapter supports existing File Handling scenarios: Fail, Ignore, and Override. It also allows multiple options for upload using Upload Type option: Media, Multi-Part, and Resumable. 
  • You can use Max Results and Page Token to customise your results while using List operation. 
  • Use Query Parameters and Response Fields to fetch desired results. 
  • Offers secure authentication via Workload Identity Federation and OAuth2 Service Account option ( uses Key Pair authentication) 
  • Supports Internet and On-Premise Connectivity options. 

How to use the Google Cloud Storage Adapter? 

The resource types available in Google Cloud Storage include buckets, folders, and objects. In this blog, we will see how to create an object of content-type image. 

Create an Object using Google Cloud Storage Adapter 

A sample of the integration flow is shown in the figure below. 

Prepare Message Details using Content Modifier 

We use a Content Modifier to store the connection details like private key alias and email. Also name of the bucket and content type of the object are stored here.

Configure Google Cloud Storage Adapter Connection tab 

The Connection tab contains connection and authentication parameters for Google Cloud Storage. The connection tab contains the following fields: 

Create Folder using Google Cloud Storage Adapter 

Now switch to Processing, to perform the Create operation to create an object of content type image. 

Verify if Folder has been created 

You can log in to Google Cloud Storage bucket to see the if the object has been created with the name img 

Quick Links 

For detailed information about Google Cloud Storage adapter configuration, see Google Cloud Storage Adapter Guide and SAP Help | Google Cloud Storage Adapter.

Note: The Google Cloud Storage Adapter is available as part of your SAP Integration Suite license.

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud – Central catalog feature revealed
entry.content:
Figure 1: SAP Business Data Cloud – Central catalog feature revealed (Source: SAP)

Introduction

The existing SAP Datasphere catalog feature allows one catalog to extract and manage metadata from multiple Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud tenants. Using this method, you can have a single catalog, though it does come with certain disadvantage

  • The user needs to select one Datasphere tenant as the primary tenant for catalog functionality
  • It also requires the user to manually set up the system connections within the catalog to execute tenant pairing and identify the necessary tenants for pairing  
  • Finally, users must ignore the catalog on paired tenants that do not serve as the main tenant. Despite being able to access certain catalog screens, they will not find asset information as it is located in the primary tenant

This blog introduces the new central catalog feature in SAP Business Data Cloud (SAP BDC) that is designed to share catalog assets across multiple systems, currently Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud systems, in your landscape (more informationhere). This feature, located within the SAP BDC cockpit, aims to unify all Datasphere catalogs in the customer's landscape. It utilizes the (UCL) Unified Customer Landscape formations defined for BDC in SAP for Me (more information here) to structure the system connections for each Datasphere catalog and the central catalog within BDC. The new central catalog feature also serves as the central place to discover data products available in the customer landscape from all SAP (e.g. S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition) and Non-SAP Providers (e.g. Databricks) and to share data products in the customer landscape to partners like Databricks.

Additionally, this blogpost demonstrates, how the BDC central catalog can be integrated into Collibra.

 

SAP BDC Central Catalog

The overall idea of the feature is depicted on the following diagram:

 

Figure 2: Interplay of BDC central catalog and Datasphere catalogs within BDC formations (Source: Own image)

In the Datasphere tenant of each BDC formation, the respective Datasphere catalog is set up along with the metadata extraction connections to systems of the respective formation. In the BDC Cockpit tenant, there is the same catalog application as in Datasphere, which provides a holistic view of all Datasphere catalogs that are within formations that contain that BDC Cockpit tenant. The catalog assets are not stored redundantly on the Datasphere catalogs but reside in the central catalog on the BDC Cockpit tenant.

To set up the central catalog, the BDC formations are configured as follows:

 

Figure 3: Overview of BDC formation setup to enable central catalog (Source: Own image)

Each BDC formation must add the same BDC Cockpit tenant. Each formation can include a Datasphere tenant (as of the General Availability Date, only greenfield Datasphere systems are included; the integration of existing Datasphere systems is planned for later in the year). Additionally, an SAP Analytics Cloud system can be added to the formation, as well as other systems like an S/4HANA system.

Figure 4: Overview of System Landscape in BDC Cockpit Tenant – Example: Formation 1 (Source: Own image)

As soon as applications are added to the BDC formation, a connection to the respective system appears in the Datasphere catalog of the formation under "Metadata Extractions" as well as on the “Metadata Extractions” tab of the central catalog on the BDC Cockpit tenant. Then, a user must be authenticated for the connection, as described here (Note: The connection can be authenticated in both the Datasphere catalog as well as the central catalog on the BDC Cockpit tenant. Only for SAP Datasphere, BW Bridge connections the user must be authenticated in the Datasphere system). Currently, assets from SAP Analytics Cloud and Datasphere can be displayed in the Datasphere catalog, as well as SAP-managed BDC data products, which are displayed from the source system (e.g. S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition).

Figure 5: System connections of BDC Formation appear in Metadata Extractions Tab in Datasphere Catalog – Example: Datasphere System of Formation 1 (Source: Own image)

The following graphic shows how the central catalog appears in the BDC cockpit tenant. In addition to the filter options available in the Datasphere catalog, it is possible to filter by BDC formations.

Figure 6: Filter options in central catalog in BDC cockpit tenant (Source: Own image)

In the following figure, it is shown how to search for SAP-managed BDC data products in the BDC cockpit tenant in the “Data Products” section.

Figure 7: Search for BDC Data Products in central catalog in BDC cockpit tenant (Source: Own image)

 

Collibra Integration with the BDC Central Catalog

Good news for the integration with Collibra: The BDC central catalog can be integrated to Collibra, similarly like SAP Datasphere. There is a blog post from Gaetan Saulnier about the integration between Datasphere and Collibra. (Note: Currently you can access Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud assets via the Collibra integration)

The only two deviations from the procedure in the blog post for integrating the central catalog with Collibra are:

1. In the BDC Cockpit Tenant, an OAuth Client with the settings purpose "API Access," access "Catalog User API," and authorization grant "Client Credentials" must be created in the "Administration" app (see following screenshot). 

Figure 8: Create OAuth Client for Collibra Integration in BDC Cockpit Tenant (Source: Own image)

2. In the "Create a Connection" dialog in Collibra, the URL of the BDC Cockpit Tenant must be entered in the "Datasphere Catalog URL" field instead of the Datasphere URL.

Figure 9: Create Connection to BDC Cockpit Tenant in Collibra via Connection Type SAP Datasphere Catalog (Source: Own image)

 The assets are displayed in Collibra grouped for each system which is connected to the central catalog. An example is shown in the following images. 

Figure 10: Systems of Formations in BDC Central Catalog displayed in Collibra (Source: Own image)

Figure 11: Datasphere Asset of Formation displayed in Collibra (Source: Own image)

 

In this blog post, you learned how easily catalog assets from various systems within a customer landscape of Business Data Cloud can be combined using the new central catalog feature. Thank you for your attention!

Thank you very much Ben, Christian, Devan, Francois, Gaetan, Mirko and Prasanthi for the collaboration on this blogpost.

Tim
